item.title: Retry intelligence — carry prior failure context into retry developer cycles
item.intent: On retry, the developer agent receives a structured summary of the prior failure — what was done and why it failed — and must acknowledge it before proceeding.
item.description: When a sprint item fails validation and retries, the developer agent currently gets the same prompt as the first attempt with no memory of what was tried. If the first attempt made a mistake (wrong approach, missed edge case), the retry is likely to repeat it. Retry intelligence captures the validation failure output and a brief summary of what was changed in the failed attempt, then injects this as 'prior failure context' into the retry developer prompt. This reduces repeat mistakes and helps the agent course-correct.
steps: After each failed validation, capture: (a) the validation output, (b) a git diff summary of what the developer changed
Accumulate failure context across retries into a structured block (attempt N: summary + failure reason)
Inject the accumulated failure context into the developer agent prompt on retry, before the item spec
The developer prompt must instruct the agent to read the failure context section and address each failure before coding
Cap injected failure context at 2000 chars to avoid prompt bloat (truncate oldest first)
Verify: run --retries 2 on an item that will fail the first validation; confirm retry prompt contains prior failure context
acceptanceCriteria: On retry, developer prompt contains a 'Prior failure context' section with attempt number, git diff summary, and validation failure output
Failure context is capped at 2000 chars and truncated gracefully
First-attempt prompt is unchanged (no failure context section)
auto mode with --retries passes failure context across all retry cycles
